g.text: Scanner Mode FSM — The current mode determines which transition rules apply to each character
'+' '-' etc. | single-char | emit from SINGLE_CHAR_TOKENS dict
other | ERROR token | emit ERROR, continue
'\n' | end of line | stop loop, leave \n for main loop → NORMAL
EOF | end of file | stop loop → NORMAL (no token emitted)
other | any char | advance() discards, stay IN_LINE_COMMENT
NOTE: newline is NOT consumed here — main loop handles it as whitespace
lexeme | exact source chars that caused the error
line | token_start_line (opening char, NOT current pos)
column | token_start_column (opening char, NOT current pos)
recovery | scanner continues — ALL errors collected, no halt
'"' | closing quote | emit STRING → back to NORMAL
esc: n/t/r/"/\\ | valid esc | consume 2 chars, stay IN_STRING
esc: other | invalid esc | emit ERROR(bad esc), stay IN_STRING
EOF | EOF in string | emit ERROR(unterminated) → NORMAL
other | any char | accumulate, stay IN_STRING
'*' then '/' | close seq | emit nothing → back to NORMAL
'*' then X | lone asterisk | discard, stay IN_BLOCK_COMMENT
other | any char | advance() discards, stay mode
NOTE: comments do NOT nest | first */ always closes
